growth_records.strain: pv. tomato DC3000 (background context, not primary measurement)
growth_records.cite: (see paper; contingency phage resistance study) et al. Historical Contingency Drives Compensatory Evolution and Rare Reversal of Phage Resistance. Molecular Biology and Evolution (msac182) (2022). [PMC9447851]
growth_records.snippet: According to previous work, the average doubling time of P. syringae at this temperature is approximately 1.27 h (Young et al. 1977).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (1.27 h at 28C) is a background literature value cited from Young et al. 1977 (not an original measurement of DC3000 by this study; medium/strain of the original source not specified here) used only to justify the 3-day transfer interval in the evolution experiment. growth_rate_per_h = ln2/1.27 = 0.546 h-1 (calculated). All other growth-rate comparisons in this paper (ancestral vs. phage-resistant strains, logistic-model mu_max) are reported only as normalized/relative values in figures, with no absolute numeric growth rates given in the text, so they could not be extracted.
